task: I’m helping a veteran’s family pull together one practical benefits note while they wait on a few moving pieces, and I want it grounded in the actual pages so I can double-check things later. Please start on DFAS and find the exact form a surviving spouse would use to begin an SBP annuity claim, then open the actual form or claim page so you can confirm the form name and number and leave that DFAS page open as proof. Once you have that survivor-benefit piece, go to Reddit and look through VA benefits_claims discussions about getting more detail on an in-progress VA claim than the normal VA.gov tracker shows, especially references to the benefits_claims API endpoint or similar methods people are using, and open the most useful discussion thread in its own tab so I can see the comments myself. After that, use Google to get to the IRS guidance on cash gifts and figure out whether receiving money from relatives is taxable, who would be responsible for any gift tax reporting, and when a gift tax return is required, because the family may need temporary help while waiting on benefits; please open the actual IRS page, not just a summary site. Then use Google again to find a reliable source explaining what happens to SNAP benefits during a U.S. federal government shutdown, so we can understand whether food assistance usually continues, whether there are timing risks or exceptions, and what the practical takeaway is for monthly planning; open the source page you rely on so I can verify it. In the end, give me one short plain-language brief that starts with the SBP form name and number, then covers the VA claim-status tip from Reddit, then the IRS cash-gift guidance, and ends with the SNAP shutdown impact.
grader_reasoning:
R1: failure - The agent attempted to navigate to the DFAS page (https://www.dfas.mil/RetiredMilitary/survivors/ApplySBP/) to identify the form. However, the `webfetch` at step 40 returned an "HTTP 403 Forbidden" error, indicating the agent was blocked from accessing the page. Although the agent claimed in step 6 to have found "DD Form 2656-7" and left the page open, the browser state at the end of the trajectory (step 42) shows that the DFAS tab was closed or inaccessible, and the agent never successfully verified the content of that page due to the 403 error. Therefore, the agent failed to provide the required proof page.

R2: success - The agent successfully identified and opened a relevant Reddit thread ("Unlock Your VA Claim Secrets: Access Hidden Data via the VA API" on r/VeteransBenefits). The agent's final brief correctly summarizes the method discussed in the thread: logging into VA.gov in the same browser session and accessing the `https://api.va.gov/v0/benefits_claims` endpoint to retrieve JSON data, including claim-specific details like estimated dates. The agent also provided the requested summary in the final brief.

R3: success - The agent successfully navigated to the official IRS guidance on gift taxes (https://www.irs.gov/businesses/small-businesses-self-employed/frequently-asked-questions-on-gift-taxes and https://www.irs.gov/faqs/interest-dividends-other-types-of-income/gifts-inheritances/gifts-inheritances-1). In the final brief, the agent correctly identified that the recipient does not owe tax on cash gifts, that the donor is responsible for any gift tax, and that a gift tax return (Form 709) is required if the gift exceeds the annual exclusion ($19,000 for 2025/2026). The information is accurate and grounded in the official IRS pages opened during the session.

R4: success - The agent successfully navigated to a reliable source (Food Research & Action Center - FRAC) regarding SNAP benefits during a federal government shutdown. The agent's brief accurately summarizes that benefits generally continue in the short term but face timing risks during prolonged shutdowns, and it provides the source page (https://frac.org/blog/how-will-government-shutdown-affect-snap-benefits) which was left open for verification.

R5: success - The agent provided a plain-language brief that follows the requested order:
1. SBP form name and number (DD Form 2656-7).
2. VA claim-status tip (using the API endpoint).
3. IRS cash-gift guidance (donor responsibility, Form 709, annual exclusion).
4. SNAP shutdown impact (potential for delays in prolonged shutdowns).

The structure is clear, concise, and covers all four required topics in the specified sequence.

The information is accurate and grounded in the official IRS pages opened during the session.
